No showdown value against Minni's polarized betting range after three barrels IP: value (AJ+, sets JJ/66/33, two-pair J6/J3/63, completed 45 wheel/straight) crushes us, bluffs (missed diamond/heart draws, KQ/AQ) possible but river As improves her Ax heavily and is a reasonable check-back/check-fold card for many medium strength hands (overpairs QQ/TT/KK now vulnerable, Jx). Check-calling flop/turn OOP with draw was correct for equity realization deep (~94BB eff vs her 165BB), but now capped/noise. Donk betting bluff has poor fold equity: our double check-call range contains too much Jx/Ax/sets/draws that continue, bloating pot OOP deep if called/raised; better to check-fold and preserve stack (94BB deep in 3-handed MTT with 74 hands left). Minni's history shows aggression but calling stations light (e.g., Hand 38 JJ v her range, Hand 55); expect her to bet value/bluffs ~60-70%, but hero-calling jack-high -EV without specific overbet bluffs. Check to induce bluffs/value thin or showdown weak value.

I was the preflop aggressor and continued on the flop and turn; Elon called both streets, which shows real willingness to continue. The river A♠ is a dangerous card for my queen-high — it improves a lot of hands in his calling range (Ax, two pair, small pairs) and cuts my already-small showdown equity. Elon checked the river, but given he called flop and turn I assign significant weight to him having at least a pair. A bluff here requires fold equity that I likely don't have, so betting risks getting called by hands that beat me. With essentially zero showdown value and no clean way to extract value, the lowest-variance, highest-expected-value line is to check and go to showdown (and fold if he fires a sizable river bet). I check to avoid throwing chips away on a thin bluff.

I am on the button with 4h 3c, which is a very weak hand. The pot is currently 150, and it's 100 to call if I want to play. Given the strength of the hand and the fact that I am against two active opponents (Elon and Minni) who have been relatively aggressive in previous hands, there is no reason to enter the pot with such poor hole cards. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity to maximize my stack.

6d2h is one of the worst hands in poker, with negligible equity against any reasonable range from Minni in the big blind. Even though it's cheap to complete the small blind, playing this hand postflop out of position with no straight or flush potential is highly -EV. Tournament setting requires preserving stack for premium spots to maximize long-term growth. Folding is the disciplined, profitable decision.

Q3 offsuit is a very weak hand preflop, especially as first to act on the button facing the blinds. No suitedness, no straight potential, dominated by any queen or better. With deep stacks and players who have shown aggression in prior hands, entering the pot would commit chips with poor equity. Best to fold and wait for a stronger spot to maximize long-term stack growth.

I’m in the small blind with pocket Aces (Ac Ah) heads-up vs the big blind (Flash, stack 11,125). Pot is 150 and it’s a clear spot to build the pot and isolate with the best starting hand. Flash has shown a willingness to call large bets, so I want a size that extracts value while still leaving room for post‑flop play. I’ll raise to 900 total (I’ve posted 50, so I add 850). If he calls (adds 800) the pot will be about 1,800 and I’ll apply straightforward value aggression on later streets. If he 3‑bets big or shoves I will commit with AA — it’s the top hand and worth maxing against his calling/3‑betting tendencies.

I am in the Big Blind holding Ten of Spades and Four of Clubs, a very weak hand. Minni, in the Small Blind, has made a significantly large raise to 900 (9x the big blind). Given the strength of my hand and the size of the raise, there is no justification for calling or raising. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

I’m on the button with 10♣8♦ and only the blinds are in (pot 150, to call 100). This is a playable, positionally advantaged hand with straight/connector potential — I don’t want to limp and invite multiway action. I’ll open-raise to 300 (3x BB) to take the initiative, apply pressure to the blinds, and build a pot I can play from position. If I face a large, polarized 3‑bet I’ll give it up; vs a small 3‑bet or cold-call from the SB I’ll proceed postflop with continuation bets on favorable textures and leverage position to extract value or fold when out of line.

I am in the Small Blind with 9s 7d. Minni, who has been active and currently holds the chip lead, has opened for 300 from the Button. While 9-7 offsuit has some speculative value in short-handed play, calling out of position against an aggressive chip leader is generally a losing play. I'll fold and wait for a better spot to utilize my stack.

In the big blind facing a 3x raise from the button (Minni, who has been aggressive in prior hands with frequent preflop raises). My hand is Ad6s (offsuit A6). Button opening range is wide (likely 30-40% of hands), so A6o blocks AA/AKs and dominates weaker Ax hands. Equity realization ~35-40% vs that range. Pot is 450, calling 200 more (pot odds 450:200 ≈ 2.25:1, requiring ~31% equity to call profitably, which we exceed). Deep stacks (92BB effective), OOP postflop but manageable with ace-high; can fold to c-bet if miss, play if hit. Folding too tight here vs button; no strong 3bet value or bluff candidate. Calling to realize equity and control pot OOP.

Top pair (sixes) with ace kicker on a 6h-3d-Jd flop, which has two diamonds creating a flush draw and possible gutshots. Heads-up OOP vs Minni's wide button opening range (~35-40%), which includes many Ax worse than mine, weaker pairs, bluffs, and draws. Equity strong (~75%+ vs range). Pot 650, deep effective stacks (~90BB). Checking first is standard OOP to pot control with one pair while disguising strength, inducing a wide c-bet from villain (who frequently cbets per history), setting up a check-raise for value/protection/fold equity against draws/overcards. Betting risks checking back equity or facing raises; check allows multiway realization or trapping weak value hands.

I opened preflop and have position with T♣8♦; flop Jd-3d-6h checked to me. I’ve missed — no pair and only one diamond (so no real flush draw) — but as the preflop aggressor on a fairly dry board I still have good fold equity vs many of Elon’s calling hands (Ax, Kx, Qx, small pairs). I’ll make a standard continuation bet to try to take the pot down and keep initiative. Betting 300 (~45% pot) is small enough to avoid bloating the pot with a marginal holding but large enough to fold out a lot of overcards and weak hands. Plan: if Elon folds I collect the pot; if he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn (continue when I pick up equity or check/control on blanks); I will respect a sizable check‑raise and fold to heavy aggression since my showdown equity is low.

Top pair top kicker (6s pairing 6h, Ad kicker) on a draw-heavy flop (Jd3d flush draw, possible gutshots). Vs Minni's wide button opening range (~35% hands) and frequent c-bet aggression, we crush Ax worse than Ad (A2-A5, A8o/A9o etc.), weaker pairs, bluffs/floats/draws. Strong equity (~75%+ vs polarized c-bet range). Pot 950, facing 300 (~1/3 pot bet, weak for value, reps wide). OOP check-raise exploits her aggression: denies equity to ~20% diamond draws (they fold or realize <25% equity calling), extracts value from worse, builds pot with our nut advantage on paired board. Call too passive OOP (vulnerable to turn draws/overcards); fold insane. Size to 900 total (~3x her bet, ~pot-committing light) for fold equity/value balance on deep stacks (~90BB eff.). History shows Minni calls light but folds to pressure when behind (e.g., Hands 14,17,22,24). Maximizes EV long-term.

I opened pre and c-bet 300 on Jd-3d-6h with T♣8♦ (one diamond, no pair). Elon’s check‑raise to 900 is large and clearly polarized toward strong value (Jx, a diamond combo, or a set) or a well-timed bluff. Pot = 1850, call = 600 → break‑even equity = 600 / (1850+600) ≈ 24.5%. My hand has only backdoor possibilities and ~6 immediate outs to a pair (~24% to pair by river) but much of the opponent’s range (top pair, diamonds) dominates or blocks my outs, so true equity vs his range is likely below the threshold. I planned to respect a sizable check‑raise and fold marginal holdings; with little fold equity left and a large raise in front of me, folding preserves a healthy stack for better spots. I’ll fold and wait for a more favorable situation.

I am holding 4h 2c, which is a very weak hand. Given my position on the button and the tendency of both Elon and Minni to play aggressively, there is no strategic advantage to entering this pot with garbage cards. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

Q9 suited is a strong speculative hand in the small blind facing only the big blind after button folds. Excellent pot odds (pot 150 to call 50 more), high playability with suited connectors, straight/flush potential, and domination potential against worse queens or suited hands. Calling completes cheaply heads-up OOP, realizes equity well with implied odds given deep stacks (mine 9975, Minni 18800). Raising OOP risks bloating pot with marginal hand; folding too tight. Standard defend wide in SB vs BB.

Elon (SB) just limped to 50 — a very wide/weak action. I'm in the BB with J♠T♥, a strong heads-up hand (good connectivity and Broadway potential). There's no cost to check and see a flop; raising here would bloat the pot and put me OOP on later streets against a likely weak range. Plan: check to realize equity and play the flop — call small c-bets with good equity/draws, raise for value or as a semi-bluff when I connect strongly, fold to large, polarized aggression when totally missed. Effective stacks are deep enough to play postflop, so keep pot control preflop.

Flop of 8s-8c-3d completely misses my Qd9d. I have queen-high, a weak backdoor diamond flush draw, but no pair, no made straight draw, and no strong equity. Board is paired, heavily favoring her checking range (any 8x, 3x, or pocket pairs). OOP in limped pot with no initiative, betting air risks raising her strong hands while folding out better bluffs. Pot 200 is small relative to stacks (mine 9925, hers 18800), so check preserves stack, induces bluffs from her wide BB range, allows cheap showdown if checked behind, and keeps multiway potential on turn. Folding to a bet will be easy given lack of equity. Standard check-fold spot, but check first to realize thin equity.

Elon limp‑called pre and then checked the flop — a very wide/weak line. I have J♠T♥ on 3♦‑8♠‑8♣: two overcards and 6 clean outs (3 J + 3 T), roughly ~24% to pair by the river. There is no real flush or straight draw and I’m out of position, so leading here would bloat the pot with only overcards and put me in a difficult spot against hands that already beat me (pocket 8s, sets, or even a 3). I’ll check to realize equity and keep pot control. Plan: call a small c‑bet (good equity vs a wide range), fold to large polarized aggression, and if I hit a J/T on the turn I’ll fire/raise for value; otherwise proceed cautiously on later streets.

Turn 9s pairs my 9d, giving me middle pair (9s) with a strong Queen kicker on a paired 8s board. Improved from complete air on flop to a solid made hand that likely beats her wide checking range in this limped BB vs SB pot (worse pairs like 88/33/77/TT, ace-high, draws, bluffs). Board has spade flush draw completing and gutshot straight draws (e.g., 10x, 7x), so betting protects against those while building the small 200 pot. OOP, betting takes initiative, gets value from weaker hands/draws, and denies her cheap equity realization. Her flop check suggests weakness or trapping, but deep stacks (9925/18800) and pot odds favor a pot-sized or 3/4 pot bet to balance value/bet sizing. Checking risks giving free cards to draws or inducing her bluffs but misses value from calling range. Bet 150 (75% pot) is well-sized for thin value/protection without overbetting into monsters.

Fold. After Elon limp-calls pre and checks the flop, his small turn stab (150 into a 350 pot → pot is 500) is asking me to commit 150 to see one card. Calling 150 requires ~23% equity (150/(500+150) ≈ 23.1%), but I only have J♠T♥ with 6 clean outs to pair the river (6/46 ≈ 13%), so this is a negative‑EV chase. A raise as a bluff would only be profitable if he folded >~50% of the time to a min‑raise, which his passive lines make unlikely. Preserve stack and wait for a better spot — fold.

Preflop on the button with 2s8h offsuit, one of the worst possible hands with minimal playability, no straight or flush potential against likely calling ranges from blinds. Opponents have shown aggression but folding conserves stack for better spots in a tournament where position is good but hand strength is not. Standard fold to avoid spew.

I’m in the small blind heads-up with Td2h — a marginal hand but perfectly playable in this spot. With only the big blind to act against, I want to take the initiative and keep a wide raising range to pressure a defending BB. A min-raise to 200 (small relative to my 18,750 stack) is the standard, low-variance way to either pick up the blind immediately or play as the aggressor postflop. If Flash calls I’ll continuation-bet many flops and fold to a large 3-bet; if he 3-bets small I’ll re-evaluate but with T2o I’m likely to fold to heavy action. Overall, raising to 200 maximizes my chance to win the pot cheaply while preserving stacks for postflop maneuvering.

I am in the Big Blind with 6c 3h. Elon has folded from the button, and Minni has min-raised to 200 from the Small Blind. Although I am getting 3:1 pot odds to call and will have position post-flop, 6-3 offsuit is an extremely weak hand with very poor playability. In a tournament setting, it is better to fold such junk hands and preserve the stack for more favorable spots, especially against the chip leader.

I’m on the button with 3♦5♣ — a weak, unsuited hand but in position against only the small and big blinds. Pot is 150 and it costs 100 to call; min raise is 200. With deep stacks and positional advantage, opening a wide button range to steal the blinds is standard. I’ll raise to 300 total (3x the big blind) — large enough to generate fold equity but not overcommit with a marginal hand. Plan: fold to a sizable 3-bet, but if called use position to c-bet favorable flops and apply pressure; control pot size when I miss.

K6o is a weak hand to defend from the Small Blind against a 3x open from an aggressive Button. Playing out of position against Minni, who has been very active and holds the chip lead, is likely to lead to a negative expected value situation. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity to contest a pot.

5h4c is a weak suited hand with poor playability out of position post-flop. Facing a button raise from aggressive Minni, the pot odds (200 to call into 450) don't justify defending this trash hand against her wide range. Equity is low, and OOP it's a clear fold to preserve stack for better spots in this deep-stack tournament.

I am on the button with Ace-Eight offsuit, which is a strong opening hand in three-handed play. I will put in a standard raise to 300 to exert pressure on the blinds and take control of the pot early, either winning it now or playing a heads-up pot in position.

Qh6c is a weak offsuit hand with poor post-flop playability out of position in the small blind. Facing a button raise from Flash, who often raises wide, but my hand has low equity against his range and is dominated by better queens or any pair. Calling 250 OOP risks difficult spots post-flop with reverse implied odds. Minni still to act behind. Folding preserves stack for better spots in this tournament phase with deep stacks.

54o is a marginal hand but I’m deep-stacked and getting decent pot odds to defend the big blind against a 3x button open. I won’t 3-bet with this weak, offsuit connector — calling keeps the pot controllable and lets me realize implied odds when I hit a disguised pair or a straight/heart backdoor. Pot is 450 and I need to put in 200 more, so my total commitment will be 300. Postflop plan: check and fold to heavy continuation bet when I miss most boards; continue aggressively only with a pair, two pair/straights, or strong draws. If Flash 3-bets to a large size I’ll usually fold this hand.

I have top pair (5s) with a very weak 4-kicker on a paired flop (3c 5h 3d). Flash opened from the button and will have a wide continuation-range; I beat a lot of his air/one-pair hands but I’m vulnerable to any 3x, and to 5x hands with a better kicker. There are essentially no strong draws to deny, so protection isn’t necessary — pot control is the priority. Checking keeps the pot manageable, lets him bluff or c-bet worse hands, and allows me to call reasonable bets for value while folding to polarization (large check-raises/turn jams). Plan: check, call small–medium c-bets to extract from worse 5x or pair hands, re-evaluate on the turn and fold to heavy aggression if I don’t improve.

I have Ace-high on a very dry, paired board (3c-5h-3d) after being the pre-flop aggressor. This board favors my range significantly, as it is unlikely to have improved Minni's big blind defending range. I will place a standard continuation bet of approximately 40% of the pot to fold out her non-pairing hands and maintain control of the pot. If she calls, I still have the best hand frequently and have overcard outs if behind a small pair.

I have top pair (5) with a very weak 4-kicker on a paired 3-5-3 flop. Flash opened preflop and is c-betting a small 250 into a 900 pot — a wide, low-effort continuation bet that many worse hands (A-x, K-x, missed broadways, small pairs) will make. There are essentially no significant draws to protect against, so pot control and extracting value is the priority. Raising here would bloat the pot with a mediocre kicker and risk folding out the hands I want to get value from; calling keeps the pot manageable and lets me fold to heavy turn aggression if I don't improve. Plan: call the small c-bet, evaluate the turn — continue vs small/medium bets for value, fold to polarized shove/large raises unless I improve to two pair/trips.

I’m holding 5c4h on 3c-5h-3d-3h — I have a full house (threes full of fives). That’s an extremely strong, disguised value hand; the only common holdings that beat me are the fourth 3 (quads) or pocket 5s (5-5 → fives full of threes). Flash’s line (button open, small flop c‑bet) is wide — he often has one-pair hands, A/K high, single 5’s, or other medium holdings that will happily call a reasonable turn bet. There are essentially no meaningful draws to protect against, so I want to extract value without scaring off caller hands.

Plan: bet for value on the turn sizing to keep worse one-pair/two‑pair and single‑5 hands in the pot while still building the pot if he’s willing. A ~2/3‑pot turn bet accomplishes that — large enough to extract but small enough to induce calls. If Flash check‑raises massively or jams, I’ll re‑evaluate (but will often call big shoves given how few hands beat me).

The board is 3c-5h-3d-3h, meaning there are three 3s on the board. I have Ace-high (Ah-8s), which currently plays as 3-3-3-A-8. Minni, after defending the big blind and calling a continuation bet on the flop, has led out for a large bet (800 into 1150) on the turn. This 'donk bet' is very polarizing. On this specific board, any pocket pair (22, 44, 66+) or any 5 (common in a BB defending range) makes a full house, which beats my Ace-high. While she could be bluffing with the three 3s as a scare card, the range of hands that call a flop and then lead this turn for value (5x, pocket pairs, or the case 3) is significantly wider than her potential bluffs. In a 3-handed game, I should be wary of over-committing with just Ace-high when the opponent shows significant aggression on a board where many marginal hands have improved to a full house. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Folded to me on the button in an unopened pot with K2 suited, a speculative hand with fold equity and position advantage. Blinds are relatively shallow compared to Minni's deep stack, but stealing blinds builds the stack over many hands. Standard raise size of 3x BB (300) offers good fold equity against likely blind defending ranges based on history of frequent folds to button raises. If called, suited king has playability post-flop in position with straight/flush potential. Folding wastes position; limping invites multiway pots with marginal hand.

Holding 8s5h in the small blind facing Elon's open to 300 (I need to call 250). This is a weak, unsuited hand with very poor playability out of position. Calling 250 into a 450 pot gives me roughly 36% pot-odds to continue — my equity against a standard button opening range (and likely BB action) is typically well below that, and I’ll often be dominated or forced to play big pots OOP. A min-raise to 500 is a weak, speculative play that unnecessarily commits chips with terrible blockers; a 3-bet shove would be an overbluff and not required with my large stack and better spots coming. I’ll preserve my deep stack and fold this marginal hand.

I am in the Big Blind with Ten-Four offsuit. Elon, on the button, has raised to 300. While the pot odds are somewhat attractive (200 to call for a 450 pot), Ten-Four is a very weak hand that performs poorly post-flop, especially out of position. Given that I am currently in second place in chip stacks and Elon's button-raising range could easily dominate such a marginal hand, I will choose to fold and wait for a better spot to commit chips.

Button with 8♦6♥ — a marginal hand but very playable in position against only the blinds. I want to take the initiative and pressure the blinds rather than limp and invite a multi-way pot. A standard 3x open (300 total) is consistent with my table image and gives more fold equity than the min-raise to 200. Plan: if I face a large 3-bet I’ll fold this hand (it doesn’t play well squeezed); if called I’ll use positional advantage to c-bet favorable flops and control pot size on dangerous textures. Raising to 300 maximizes expected value here while keeping the pot manageable with a speculative holding.

I am in the Small Blind with Ten-Four suited. While the hand is suited and we are playing 3-handed, Ten-Four is still a very weak hand to defend against a raise from an aggressive player like Minni, especially since I will be out of position for the remainder of the hand. With a stack of over 100 big blinds, there is no need to commit chips to a marginal or sub-marginal spot. Folding is the most prudent play to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

Q2 offsuit is a very weak hand with no straight or flush potential, dominated by any queen or better in Minni's likely button opening range. Defending out of position preflop would be a mistake as it plays poorly post-flop. Pot odds are ~2.25:1 but equity realization OOP is low. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots.

I am on the button with T3o, which is a very weak hand. While being on the button in a three-handed game allows for a wider opening range, T3o is significantly below the threshold for a profitable open. Given that Minni (the big blind) has been quite active and Elon (the small blind) is also capable of defending, there is no reason to waste chips on a low-equity steal attempt.

3-handed play with button (Flash) folding preflop makes this effectively heads-up SB vs BB. K3o is a standard open-raise from the SB in this spot as it is within the top ~60% of hands typically raised 3-handed or heads-up. King-high provides decent high-card strength, playability for top pair, and acts as a steal attempt on the dead money (pot already 150). Deep effective stacks (~100BB), but min-raise or small raise is low variance and +EV given Minni's history of calling wide but folding to aggression at times. Opting for a standard 2.5x BB size (250 total) to build pot while maintaining fold equity. Limping OOP is suboptimal; folding too tight.

Elon opened to 250 from the SB (about 2.5bb). I have 4♠3♣ — a very weak offsuit hand and I’ll be out of position postflop, but the pot is 350 and my required additional call is 150 (250 total), giving ~3.3:1 pot odds (need ≈23% equity). Elon has shown a fairly wide opening frequency, so defending the BB with a call is the most +EV line here — I won’t 3-bet with this hand because it’s too weak OOP and Elon often continues. Plan: call 250 to see a flop; fold to heavy aggression on dry boards unless I hit a pair or a real draw, and play aggressively only if I make a meaningful hand or get a clear read.

Flop of 6c-Qc-Kc is extremely coordinated with three clubs to a flush and top pair possible for both players. Holding Kh3d gives top pair with a terrible 3d kicker (loses to any Kx where x>Q, plus any two clubs for a flush, Qx, or better). Preflop, K3o was a standard SB steal/raise, but Minni's BB call suggests a wide defending range including suited broadways, Ax suited, Kx suited, suited connectors, pairs - many of which connect strongly here (e.g., Qx, KcXx, clubs). OOP first to act deep-stacked (~100BB), betting (donk) risks isolating vs better or getting raised off equity. Checking extracts value from worse (e.g., Qx, bluffs) if she bets small or checks behind weaker hands, while allowing fold to aggression without committing further. High fold equity on turn/river if checked through. Prioritizes pot control and avoiding tough spots with marginal showdown value on scary board.

I defended preflop with 4♠3♣ and the flop comes Kc Qc 6c. I currently have one club (3♣) and there are three clubs on board, so I’m effectively one card short of a club flush (9 outs) — roughly ~35% to make a flush by the river. I’m out of position vs the preflop raiser (Elon) and he checked the flop. Betting here as a pure semi-bluff has limited fold equity on a mono‑club board (he raised preflop and his range likely contains a lot of clubs and strong hands), and a c-bet would commit chips OOP into a likely calling/raising range. My preflop plan was to be passive with this hand unless I hit; this flop gives me a draw but not a made hand. Best line is to check and realize equity for free: if Elon bets a small/medium size on the turn I’ll frequently call with my draw; I’ll fold to large turn raises unless I hit a club or pair; if a club hits I can start betting for value or even check‑raise depending on sizing. If Elon checks back I’ll see the river free. This minimizes risk while preserving my ~35% chance to improve and keeps pot control OOP.

Turn 10s adds straight draw potential (J9, 9J) but doesn't complete the nut flush draw, keeping the board scary but my Kh3d as top pair with a weak kicker ahead of Qx, Ax-high, smaller pairs, and missed draws in Minni's wide BB defending range that checked flop. Her flop check polarizes her range somewhat weaker (less likely KK/QQ/set/flush), favoring thin value betting to extract from Qx/draws/bluffs while denying equity to JT/9J/JT suited clubs. OOP deep-stacked (~100BB eff), small pot-sized bet (60% pot) balances protection, value, and fold equity without overcommitting; can fold to raise if she shows strength. Checking risks giving free card to draws or checked-down showdown where kicker loses, suboptimal for maximizing EV in tournament spot. History shows Minni calls stations but vulnerable to aggression post-check.

Board Kc Qc 6c Ts, my cards 4s3c — I have one club (3c) and need one club on the river to make a flush: 9 outs => 9/46 ≈ 19.6% to hit the river. Elon opened wide preflop, checked the flop and now fires a relatively small turn bet (300 into a 800 pot). Pure pot‑odds: I must call 300 to win 1100, so I need ≈27.3% equity — the raw math says a call on equity alone is slightly negative. However Elon’s line and small sizing indicate a wide range that includes many hands I can realize equity against, and if I hit I can often extract value (though I must respect A♣/K♣/Q♣ combos that already beat me). Raising as a semi‑bluff OOP with a one‑card draw is too high variance and likely to be called or shoved by clubs; folding surrenders decent equity against a wide opponent. Plan: call the 300 to see the river, then fold to heavy action if I miss, and if I hit the club river look to get value while being cautious of higher‑club blockers.

River 5s completes the spade flush draw (Ts and 5s), adding another layer of danger to an already scary board (Kc Qc 6c pre-turn). Holding top pair Kh with a terrible 3d kicker, my hand has very poor showdown value against Minni's BB defending range, which includes better kickers on Kx (KQ, KT, KJ), any two clubs for flush, Qx combinations, sets, and two pairs. No straight possible (5-6-T-Q-K gaps). Preflop wide call + flop check-behind + turn call polarizes her somewhat: likely missed club draws now with spades? or medium strength like Qx, weaker Kx, Ax-high. OOP first to act on river in 1100 pot with deep stacks (~100BB eff). Value betting thin risks getting called by better or raised off equity; overbetting folds out bluffs/draws we beat. Checking induces bluffs from missed draws (club/spade flush attempts, gutshots), extracts thin value bets from Qx/A-high, controls pot, and preserves stack for better spots. History shows frequent check-downs and Minni vulnerable post-check aggression, but here we prioritize avoiding tough hero-call/fold spots with marginal hand. Check for pot control and showdown if she checks weaker.

I missed my flush and have no pair or meaningful showdown value on Kc Qc 6c Ts 5s with 4♠3♣. Elon opened wide preflop, checked the flop, then made a small turn stab (300) that I called with my draw — that line is consistent with a wide range (pairs, K/Q, some clubs, and some bluffs). He now checks the river; that can be either pot‑control with a marginal made hand or a trap with a club, but either way I’m out of position with nothing. Betting as a bluff here has low expected value: many hands in his range (Kx/Qx and any club) will call or raise, and a raise would put me to an extremely difficult decision with no equity. With a 1.9-to-1 reward-to-risk (pot ≈1100) and a near-zero chance to be best, the lowest-variance +EV line is to check and concede if he fires — preserve 19,200 chips rather than risking them for a marginal steal. Plan: check (see a showdown for free); fold to any river bet.

3-handed on the button first to act preflop with A4o, a strong hand in this spot due to ace blocker and position. Standard wide opening range includes A-x offsuit from button (~40-50% hands). Raising builds the pot, takes initiative, and leverages fold equity against blinds. Opponents have shown tendencies to fold to button opens frequently in history. Raising to 3x BB (300) is standard sizing for value and playability postflop. Limp invites multiway pots which is suboptimal; fold too tight.

Elon opened to 300 from the button. I’m in the small blind with Q♦7♣ — an offsuit queen with a weak kicker that’s frequently dominated by a standard button raising range (AQ, KQ, Qx, broadways, pairs). Calling 250 to play out of position in a likely multiway pot (the big blind still acts) gives me poor post‑flop playability and bad implied odds. 3‑betting as a bluff risks isolating against shorter stacks who can shove, which I don’t want to do with this hand. With a healthy stack (19,150) it’s better to conserve chips and wait for a cleaner spot. Fold.

I am holding Ace-King of spades in the big blind, which is a premium hand. Elon raised to 300 from the button, a standard opening move. In a three-handed game, AKs is exceptionally strong. I will 3-bet to 1000 to build value and take the initiative in the hand, as I want to put pressure on a wide button opening range.

3-handed, deep stacks (~100BB). A4o on button facing BB 3-bet to 1000 (3.3x over my 300 open) after SB fold. Excellent pot odds: calling 700 more into 1350 pot (~2:1, 33% equity needed, but we have more). A4o blocks AA/AK, plays well IP with position for postflop flexibility—can flop nut flush draws, wheel straights, ace pairs (domination risk but fold equity OOP helps). Villain Flash aggressive preflop from history (frequent raises, some light), BB 3-bet range polarized: value (JJ+, AKo/ATs+, AQs) + bluffs (suited broadways/connectors). Estimated equity ~35-40% vs that range heads-up. Folding too tight preserves stack but misses EV; shoving overbet risky with marginal hand; small 4-bet induces shoves from value or folds bluffs but exposes to 5-bet jams. Flat preferred: control pot OOP, realize equity IP, exploit history of villain checking postflop often. Long-term stack maximization favors playing strong position spots.

As the pre-flop 3-better, I have the range advantage with high overpairs (AA, KK, QQ) which are unlikely to be in Elon's calling range. The board is 6h-7h-6c, which is quite dry for my perceived range but could have connected with Elon's medium pairs or suited connectors. I will lead with a continuation bet of 800 (approximately 39% of the pot) to maintain the initiative, put pressure on his overcards (like AQ or AJ), and protect my equity. AK-high still has significant value here, and a bet allows me to define the hand strength early.

A♣4♦ completely whiffs the 6♥7♥6♣ flop: no pair, no straight draw (gutshot absent), no flush draw, pure Ace-high. Preflop called BB 3-bet OOP with wide range for position/realization, but this board hits villain's polarized 3-bet range hard (sets 66/77, two-pair 76s/67s, combo draws ♥️ like A♥x/5♥x/8♥x, overpairs). Villain's 800 into ~2050 (~40% pot) small C-bet likely value/delayed or strong draw, continues most range. Equity <20% vs that (A-high dominated, folds to turns/river bets). Pot odds 800 into 2850 (~22% needed post-call) not met. Raising bluff risky: villain effective stacks ~84BB, sizing small invites calls/draws, history shows aggression but pots won with bets. IP advantage diminished with no showdown/equity. Folding preserves 95BB stack (9575) for superior spots in 3-handed play; spewing here suboptimal for long-term stack max over 150 hands.

I’m on the button with K♥9♦ (deep stack ~191 bb) in a 50/100 game. In 3-handed play my opening range should be wide and K9o is a standard open — position gives me a postflop edge and the chance to steal the blinds. The pot is 150 and it costs 100 to see the flop, so I’ll raise to 300 (a standard 2.5–3x BB size at this table) to take the initiative, narrow fields, and build a pot I can play in position. If I face a small 3-bet I’ll evaluate based on sizing and opponent; I’ll fold to a large 3-bet and proceed postflop with c-bets on favorable textures and selective continuation lines.

I am in the Small Blind with 8-7 offsuit. The button, Minni, has raised to 3bb (300). While 8-7 is a connected hand, playing it offsuit out of position against a large-stacked, aggressive opponent like Minni is generally unprofitable. Furthermore, Elon in the Big Blind is still to act and could potentially squeeze. Given the tournament stage and my current stack size of 112bb, there is no need to defend such a marginal hand from the worst position on the table.

9h6d is a very weak offsuit hand with low connectivity and no high card value. Facing a button raise to 300 (3x BB) from Minni, who has been aggressive in prior hands, her range is wide but still contains many hands with better playability and equity than mine. Defending out of position in the BB with this trash hand risks difficult post-flop situations, poor implied odds, and unnecessary stack commitment early in the hand. Standard fold to preserve chips for stronger spots in a tournament setting where ICM and survival matter over 150 hands.

I am on the button with Ten-Deuce offsuit (Tc 2h). This is a very weak hand, and even in a three-handed game, it falls well below the standard range for a profitable opening raise. Given the stacks and the activity of the blinds (especially Minni, who has the big stack and is playing very aggressively), there is no incentive to attempt a steal or play this hand out of position post-flop. I will fold.

Preflop in SB with 9c6d offsuit, a weak speculative hand. Flash has folded the button, leaving heads-up vs Minni in BB. I've posted SB 50, pot 150, calling 50 more offers excellent implied odds at 3:1 for a cheap flop. Folding dead money is suboptimal; raising this trash is not GTO as it's easily dominated. Limping mixes well, sees flop in position effectively postflop if checked to, and exploits Minni's history of passive play in some spots. Deep stacks support playing small ball.

Elon (SB) just limped; I’m in the BB with 9♠T♠ — a suited, connected hand that plays very well heads-up. With deep stacks and to-call = 0, I want to seize initiative and isolate the limper. Raising to 300 (standard preflop iso size) achieves that: it gives fold equity vs weak holdings, builds a pot I can play postflop, and only commits a small portion of my 19,200 stack. Plan: if Elon folds I scoop the blinds; if called I’ll continuation-bet frequently on favorable textures (spade/T/9/connected flops) and use pot control on dry or overcard-heavy boards. If Elon 3-bets large (polarized), I’ll heavily favor folding and re-evaluate vs smaller, more likely value or squeeze sizes.

Facing a raise to 300 total after limping SB with 9c6d offsuit, a very weak, uncoordinated hand OOP heads-up vs Minni's BB range. Pot odds are about 3:1 (calling ~250 into 600), but massive reverse implied odds due to domination risk (e.g., vs 97, A9, K9 suited, etc.), poor postflop playability OOP with low equity realizations, and high chance of being outdrawn or outplayed. Minni's frequent PF raises suggest wide range, but doesn't change that 96o is a clear fold OOP vs 3x over limp. No value to raising junk. Prioritizing stack preservation in long tournament over speculative OOP call with trash.

3-handed on the button preflop with T4o (offsuit but ten-high). Pot has 150 (blinds/limp), to call 100. Standard to raise wide from button in short-handed play to steal blinds and take initiative with position. T4o blocks some hands, has straight potential. Minni (SB) and Flash (BB) have been calling stations in history, but position advantage is huge. Raise small to 2.5x BB for value/protection. Risk-reward favors aggression to build stack.

I’m in the small blind with A♣5♥ facing a button open to 250 (2.5x). Pot is 400 and I must put in 200 more (total 250) to call. In this 3-handed spot the button’s opening range is wide and A5o has decent defensive value and an Ace-blocker — so folding is too tight. I don’t want to 3-bet as a bluff here because the big blind (Flash) has shown willingness to squeeze/call and 3-betting dominated Aces can get me in trouble multiway and out of position. Calling keeps the pot controlled, preserves postflop maneuverability, and lets me evaluate favorable flops (play aggressively when I hit or have good backdoor equity, pot-control or fold to heavy resistance on Ace-high boards). Stack depth (≈193 BBs) allows a cheap flop and postflop play. Action: call to 250.

I am in the Big Blind holding Ace-Ten offsuit (As Td). Elon (Button) raised to 250, and Minni (Small Blind) called. The pot is currently 600, and it costs me only 150 to call, giving me excellent pot odds (4:1). While ATo is a strong hand in a three-handed game, 3-betting out of position against two active players might bloat the pot unnecessarily. By calling, I close the pre-flop action and get to see a flop in a three-way pot with a hand that often dominates their ranges.

I have A♣5♥ on 5♠ A♥ 2♠ — top two pair in a three‑way pot. BTN opened and BB called, so their ranges include lots of Ax, 5x, pocket pairs and spade combos. With two opponents and two spades on the board I want to both extract value from worse Aces/5x/pairs and deny correct pot odds to spade‑draws. Betting for value/protection is best here rather than checking and giving the button a free initiative. I’ll bet roughly 2/3 pot to charge draws and get called by worse hands; if faced with a large raise I’ll re‑evaluate for a set but I’m happy to call reasonable raises with top two pair.

I have top pair (Aces) with a Ten kicker and hold the Ace of Spades, which acts as a key blocker to the nut flush. Minni has led out for 500 into a 750 pot (a 'donk bet'). This lead often represents an Ace, a set, or a strong draw. While my hand is strong, raising here might isolate me against better hands like AK, AQ, or sets, while calling allows me to keep the pot manageable and see how Elon (the original pre-flop raiser) reacts behind me. Folding is not an option given the strength of top pair in a three-handed game.

Preflop button raise with T4o as a standard steal in 3-handed play. Flop 5s Ah 2s completely misses: only gutshot to wheel straight (needing a 3, 4 outs), backdoor spade flush draw (one spade), and a 10 overcard. Board is extremely wet with two spades (strong flush draws), Ace-high, and wheel straight draw potential. Minni (SB defender) donk-bets 500 into ~750 pot, Flash (BB) calls--indicating strong ranges that connect well (Ax, sets, flush draws, pairs like 55/22/AA). My equity is low (~15-20% max vs. their ranges, as Ax dominates and flush draws have live equity). Pot odds ~3.5:1 (500 to win 2250) require ~18% equity, but multiway OOP vs. aggression, implied odds poor if I hit. Raising has fold equity but risky vs. calling stations from history and capped ranges. Best to fold weak draw, preserve deep stack (9125), and exploit position in future spots. Long-term EV positive discipline over spew.

I have A♣5♥ — two pair (Aces & Fives) on 5♠ A♥ 2♠ K♣. Flash called my flop raise, so his calling range includes lots of Ax, Kx, medium pocket pairs and some double‑spade combos (the main real threat that beats me are sets: 55 or 22). The King on the turn doesn't change that I'm ahead of most of his range, and I want to both extract value from worse Aces/Kx/pairs and deny correct odds to spade draws. Pot is 1750; a bet of ~2/3 pot charges draws (a caller would need ~40.7% equity to profitably call 1200 into 2950, well above a single‑card flush chance ~19%) while still getting called by worse made hands. Plan: bet 1200 to protect and extract value; if I face a large, polarized raise I'll re‑evaluate (small raises I will often call, very large overbets likely indicate sets and will be treated more cautiously).

I have top pair with an Ace and a Ten kicker. The King on the turn is a potential scare card, but given Minni's history of aggression and the fact that she didn't 3-bet pre-flop, hands like AK or AQ are less likely (though not impossible). My call on the flop with the Ace of Spades blocker was correct, and I am still getting decent odds to see if she slows down on the river or to catch a bluff. Folding top pair against an active player in a 3-handed game is too tight, and raising would only isolate me against hands that beat me. I will call to keep the pot manageable and evaluate the river.

I have A♣5♥ on 5♠ A♥ 2♠ K♣ 6♦ — top two pair. Very few hands beat me (mainly trips: AA/55/22/KK and a limited number of A+K or A+6 two‑pair combos); Flash called my flop and turn so his range is heavy in Ax, Kx and medium pairs with some missed spade draws. Betting here extracts thin value from worse Aces, Kx and medium pairs and charges missed spade draws that can’t call large bets profitably. I’ll bet ~2/3 pot (≈2800) to get value while still protecting my hand. If he check‑raises big (polarized/pot shove) I’ll treat it as a set/monster and fold; I’ll be willing to call small/medium raises but won’t bloat the pot without a read that he’s bluffing.

I am holding top pair (Aces) with a Ten kicker on a board of 5s-Ah-2s-Kc-6d. Minni has taken an extremely aggressive line, starting with a donk-lead on the flop and following up with significant bets on the turn and river. While my Ace-Ten was a strong hand on the flop, the King on the turn is a scare card that could have easily improved her range if she was floating with broadway cards or if she slow-played AK pre-flop (though AK usually 3-bets). More importantly, her line of leading into two players and triple-barreling is heavily weighted toward value hands like sets (55, 22) or two-pair (A5, A2). Since I hold the Ace of Spades, I block some of her potential missed flush-draw bluffs (like AsQs or AsJs), making it even more likely she is betting for value. In a 3-handed game at this stage of the tournament, calling off nearly 30% of my stack with a one-pair hand against such strength is too risky. I will fold and preserve my stack for a better spot.

I have 3♣5♠ — a weak, unconnected offsuit hand — but I’m on the button facing only the blinds. Pot is 150 and it’s only 100 to call. Given my healthy stack (21,600) and positional advantage, I want to take the initiative and generate fold equity rather than play passively. A standard button open to 300 (my usual sizing at this table) puts pressure on the SB and BB and often wins the blinds outright. If either opponent 3-bets big (especially Elon, who has shown frequent aggression), I’ll fold — this hand has poor playability versus a polarized 3-bet. If called, I’ll play postflop in position: c-bet on dry, favorable textures and exercise pot control or check-back on highly coordinated boards. Calling is too passive and a min-raise to 200 gives less fold equity, so I raise to 300 to maximize expected value while staying prepared to fold to strong preflop aggression.

I am holding 7-4 offsuit in the Small Blind. This is an extremely weak hand. Minni has raised from the Button, and with a significant chip lead, she is likely applying pressure. Even though I am getting decent pot odds to call, playing 7-4o out of position against the chip leader is a recipe for losing more chips. I will preserve my stack of 9,225 for a better spot.

Pocket 77 is a premium hand in the big blind facing a button raise from Minni, who has been aggressive with frequent raises throughout the history. Heads-up now after Flash folds. Calling gives good pot odds (200 into 650), but 77 has great equity against Minni's wide button opening range (dominates Ax, suited broadways, etc.). 3-betting isolates, builds the pot for value, and denies equity to overcards. Standard 3-bet size of 3x the raise (900 total) with deep stacks. Folding is too tight, shoving too aggressive early in tournament.

I opened to 300 with 3♣5♠ to steal the blinds but planned to fold to a sizable 3‑bet. Elon3‑bet to 900, which polarizes his range toward value and strong bluffs — but my hand is very weak and is frequently dominated or has poor equity. Calling 600 here gives marginal pot odds and forces difficult post‑flop decisions despite being in position; a 4‑bet bluff is too risky against an opponent who shows aggression and likely to call. Preserve stack for better spots — fold.

While Ten-Trey suited has some potential, it is generally too weak to open-raise from the button in a three-handed game, especially with an aggressive chip leader (Minni) in the Big Blind and an active player (Elon) in the Small Blind. I will fold and wait for a better spot to utilize my position.

82o is one of the worst hands in poker, with no high card value, no suitedness, and poor connectivity. In the small blind facing the big blind, while we have position advantage post-flop if we call, the hand plays terribly after the flop in most scenarios. Folding preserves our stack for better spots, avoiding difficult decisions out of position with a weak range. Raising would be overly loose and exploitable, and calling commits chips to a hand unlikely to improve profitably. With deep stacks and 58 hands left, patience maximizes EV.

4d2c is an extremely weak offsuit hand with negligible high card strength, poor connectivity, and minimal playability even in position on the button. Facing only blinds in an unraised pot, limping would commit chips to multiway pots where this hand struggles against any aggression or decent flops. Folding preserves the stack for better opportunities, aligning with GTO principles for trash hands in early position relative to the blinds.

I’m in the small blind with K♦5♣. Button (Elon) folded, so it’s heads-up vs Flash in the big blind. Pot is 150 and I only need to put in 50 more (100 total) to see the flop; minimum raise would be to 200 total. K5o is a marginal, often-dominated hand and I’ll be out of position postflop — raising to 200 will frequently be called by a sticky big blind and simply bloats the pot unnecessarily. The pot odds to call are attractive (~3:1) so I’ll complete and see a cheap flop; plan to continue aggressively only if I hit (top pair, good draw) and otherwise fold to strong aggression.

I am in the Big Blind with 7s 3h, which is a very weak hand. Minni completed from the Small Blind after Elon folded on the button. Since I have the option to see a free flop, I will check. There is no reason to raise with such a poor hand, especially against a deep-stacked chip leader who has been quite active.

Flop: 3c Jc 8c — I hold K♦5♣, so I have a strong club draw (9 outs to the flush) plus a K overcard. That gives me meaningful equity (~35% to hit the flush by river, plus some extra equity from the king). It’s been checked to me and I’m out of position, so betting here has limited fold equity — many hands that continue will have a club or a pair and will call. I prefer to check to control the pot, realize my draw for a free/cheap card, and see how Flash chooses to proceed. If Flash fires a small-to-medium c-bet I’ll call for the pot odds; if he checks back I get a free turn; if he jams or sizes very large I can fold and preserve a healthy stack. Action: check to keep options and capitalise on my draw equity.

I have bottom pair with a very weak kicker on a monotone board. Since Minni checked, I will check behind to keep the pot small and see a free turn. There's no value in betting here, as any better hand will call and I don't want to be check-raised on this board.

Turn: K♠ pairs my K♦ — I’ve improved to top pair but the board is dangerous (three clubs on flop). Flash checked the flop and again on the turn, so his range is wide: missed club draws, weak pairs (Jx/8x/3x), or slowplayed strong hands (possible flopped flush). I want to extract value from worse pairs and charge club draws while keeping the pot manageable. A value/protection bet of about 3/4 pot (~150 into 200) achieves that: it gets called by Jx/8x/other single-pair hands and forces pure club draws to pay a price that reduces their immediate equity. If I face a large check-raise I will fold (my kicker is weak and the board favors made flushes); if called I’ll re-evaluate on the river — proceed for value on a blank, proceed cautiously (or fold to heavy action) if a club completes the flush.

I have bottom pair with a very weak kicker on a board with three clubs and a King. Minni is betting 150 into a 200 pot. My hand is extremely vulnerable to any club, any higher pair, or even just high cards. There is very little potential for my hand to improve to a winner, and I am likely already crushed by Minni's range. It's best to fold and preserve my stack for a better spot.

Button with Q♦7♥, deep stacks (~21.4k) and blinds 50/100. Pot is 150 and it’s cheap to try a steal — Q7o is marginal but the button is the right spot to apply pressure. I’ll open to a standard 3x BB (300) to get fold equity and to isolate; if I face a significant 3-bet I’ll fold this hand, but if I get called I can use position to c-bet favorable flops or control the pot against multiple callers. 300 is a clear, conventional sizing that avoids giving opponents too-good odds to see flops.

I am dealt 3c 3s in the Small Blind. Minni has raised to 300 from the Button. While pocket pairs are often worth playing, 33 is quite weak and highly vulnerable to overcards. I am out of position against Minni, who currently has the largest stack and has been playing aggressively. Elon is in the Big Blind and could also three-bet. Calling 250 more to see a flop is acceptable given the implied odds if I set-mine (hit a three), but since I am short-handed and out of position, I will simply call and see how the flop develops.

8d5c is a trash hand: offsuit, disconnected low cards with poor playability. Facing a button raise from aggressive Minni and a SB call from Flash, entering a multiway pot out of position post-flop is disastrous. Pot odds are ~3.5:1 but equity realization is too low OOP with this hand. Raising has no fold equity and commits too much stack. Standard fold to preserve stack for premium spots in tournament.

On this flop of Ten-Jack-Deuce, my pocket threes are very vulnerable as there are two overcards to my pair. Minni was the pre-flop aggressor and this board likely connects well with her opening range from the button. Being out of position, I will check to see how she responds. If she bets, I will likely have to fold given the strength of the board and the fact that I only have a small pair with very few ways to improve.

I was the preflop aggressor and we’re now heads-up on a relatively dry Tc‑Jd‑2s flop. I have Q♦7♥ — a single overcard and a tiny backdoor diamond — so this is a good spot to take the initiative and fold out missed hands and small pairs. A standard half‑to‑two‑thirds pot continuation bet keeps pressure on the caller, preserves fold equity and gives me a clear plan if called (caution on a raise, fold to large raises). Bet ~400 into the 700 pot to represent a strong range while keeping pot control and the option to barrel the turn if appropriate.

I am holding pocket threes on a board with two overcards, a Ten and a Jack. This flop likely connects well with Minni's button opening range, containing hands like QJ, JT, or even high cards that have significant equity against me. Since I am out of position and my hand has very little potential to improve unless I hit one of the two remaining threes, calling a bet of 400 into a 700 pot is not profitable. I will fold to preserve my stack.

I am on the button with Ace-Nine suited, which is a very strong hand in 3-handed play. I will open with a standard raise to 300 to take control of the pot and exert pressure on the blinds. This hand has excellent playability post-flop and high card strength.

J3s is a weak speculative hand out of position in the small blind facing a button raise. Button ranges are wide but still crush this hand preflop, and playing OOP postflop is disadvantageous with poor equity and playability. Pot odds to call 250 into 450 are about 1.8:1, requiring over 30% equity which this hand lacks against a typical button opening range. 3-betting is not justified with such a weak hand. Folding preserves stack for better opportunities in a tournament setting where survival and position matter.

Flash opened to 300 from the button. I’m in the big blind with 9♥5♥. Calling costs me 200 more (300 total committed including my 100 BB). Pot is 450, so I’m getting ~31% pot odds (200 to win 650). Against a button open my hand has reasonable playability (backdoor flush/straight and some pair potential) and good implied odds heads-up with deep stacks (Flash ~8.5k). 3-betting as a bluff is possible but unnecessary and risky with this raggy suited hand. Folding would be too tight given the price. Plan: call and see a flop — continue on heart/connected boards or a pair, fold to big aggression on dry Ace/face c-bets with no equity.

I called preflop with 9h5h to see a flop and realized I would only continue with a heart/connected board or if I paired. Flop Kc Js 6h gives me no pair — just a weak backdoor (runner-runner) heart draw (≈4.2% to complete by the river) and 6 outs to a pair (≈24% to hit by the river). I’m out of position vs the preflop raiser, and leading out with a donk-bet with this raggy holding is unnecessary and often costly. Checking keeps the pot small, lets Flash reveal his intentions, and allows me to realize equity cheaply. Plan: check and see what Flash does — if he checks back I get a free turn; if he bets I’ll call small-to-medium sized continuation bets to see turn (folding to large, polarizing raises that represent a strong K/J or set).

On this King-high, Jack-high board, I hold Ace-high with some backdoor potential. As the pre-flop aggressor, I have a range advantage that includes strong Kings (AK, KQ) and Jacks (AJ). Since Minni checked to me, I will place a continuation bet of roughly half the pot to maintain pressure and potentially fold out middle-to-low pocket pairs or miscellaneous air in her range. If she calls, I still have some equity to improve or can potentially check down or bluff later streets depending on the turn card.

I checked the flop with 9♥5♥ and Flash c-bets 325 into 975 (~1/3 pot). Calling 325 requires ~25% equity (325 / 1300). My hand has ~6 outs to pair (~24% to hit by river) plus ~4% runner‑runner heart completion — roughly ~27–28% equity versus a reasonably wide continuation range. On the button Flash will c‑bet a lot of missed broadways and bluffs, so this small bet is consistent with a fairly wide, continuation-heavy range. I’m out of position so I won’t bluff‑raise with rag hearts; calling is the lowest-variance way to realize equity and use my deep stack for implied odds. Plan: call to see the turn; if I pair or pick up a heart I can proceed, but I’ll fold to large, polarized aggression (turn check‑raise or big shove) that represents K/J/sets or strong two‑pair.

Board Kc Js 6h 8s — I hold 9h5h: no pair and the runner‑runner heart draw is now dead. My clean outs are 3 nines + 3 fives + 4 sevens = 10 outs (~10/46 ≈ 21.7% to improve on the river). Flash’s small flop c‑bet can include Kx/Jx but also many bluffs; however I’m out of position, pot is already 1,300 and stacks are deep. I don’t want to bloat the pot OOP with a marginal hand that’s likely reverse‑dominated by a lot of his range. I’ll check to control the pot and see what Flash does on the turn; if he fires a smallish double‑barrel (~325) I may call given the pot odds, but I’ll fold to larger, polarized aggression. If he checks back I get a free river and can reassess.

The turn 8s does not significantly alter the board texture or improve my hand. I currently hold Ace-high with no draws. Minni called my continuation bet on the flop, which indicates she likely has a piece of the board, such as a Jack or a weak King, or perhaps a straight draw like QT. Betting a second barrel here is risky; Minni is the chip leader and has shown a willingness to see hands through. If I bet and she calls, I will be in a difficult spot on the river with almost no equity. By checking back, I control the size of the pot and give myself a chance to see a free river card, where an Ace could potentially give me the best hand or I might win a small pot if she was on a draw.

I check. On the river my 9♥5♥ has no showdown value — no pair, no flush/straight — and the backdoor heart is dead. Flash’s line (button open preflop, small c-bet on K‑J‑6, then check on the turn) is consistent with a wide range that includes Kx/Jx, medium pairs, and some turn/called bluffs. His turn check reduces my fold equity on the river, so bluffing out of position with no real equity is high variance and likely to be called by top/middle pair. Pot is only 1,300 and stacks are deep, so the lowest-variance, exploitative play is to check and concede the initiative — I’ll fold to any value-sized river bet and only consider calling an extremely tiny probe. Checking preserves chips and keeps my losses small when I’m almost certainly behind.

On the river, the board is Kc Js 6h 8s 4s. I have Ace-high (Ad 9d). Minni has checked to me for the third time this hand. Given that she called my flop continuation bet but we both checked the turn, it is unlikely she has a very strong hand like a top King, which she likely would have bet for value or protection by now. My Ace-high has decent showdown value against her missed draws (such as QT or T9). Betting here is unlikely to get a better hand (like a Jack) to fold, and I am unlikely to get called by any hand I currently beat. Therefore, checking back to realize my showdown value is the most prudent play.

Pocket eights is a premium hand heads-up or 3-handed, especially from the button with position on both opponents. Minni in SB is deep-stacked and aggressive historically, Flash in BB also plays pots but has folded to raises before. Raising builds the pot for value, applies pressure on blinds, and allows me to play post-flop in position. Limping is weak and invites multiway pots; folding is too tight. Standard button open-raise size of 3x BB (300 total) balances protection and fold equity while keeping pot manageable with my stack of ~93BB.

Button opened to 300 and I’ve already posted 50 from the SB, so calling 250 would make my total in the pot 300. I hold Qc6c — a marginal but playable suited queen with backdoor flush/draw potential. With three players and deep stacks, I don’t want to bloat the pot out of position by 3‑betting; a small reraise is likely to get me 3‑bet or called and leave me OOP in a big pot. The immediate call gives decent pot odds to see a flop and allows me to continue only with meaningful equity (top pair, strong draws) and fold to heavy aggression when missed. I’ll defend and evaluate the flop rather than force the action preflop.

I am in the Big Blind with Ace-Nine offsuit. Elon raised from the Button and Minni called from the Small Blind. The pot currently stands at 700, and I only need to call 200 more to see a flop. This gives me pot odds of 3.5:1. Ace-Nine is a strong hand in a 3-handed game and performs well against the Button's wide opening range. While a 3-bet squeeze is an option, calling is a solid, lower-variance play that allows me to see a flop with a hand that has significant high-card value and some blocking power against other Ace-X hands. I will call to close the pre-flop action.

I have Qc6c on Qd-3c-Js: top pair but a weak kicker, and I’m out of position against both the button (Elon) and the BB (Flash). The flop is relatively dry (one club, no obvious straight draws), so a small value/protection bet is best — it charges worse queens/Jx and takes away free cards, while keeping the pot controllable OOP. I’ll make a 1/3‑pot bet to extract thin value and get info; if I face a large raise I’ll fold given my weak kicker, but if called I’ll re-evaluate on the turn.

I am in the Big Blind with Ace-Nine offsuit on a flop of Queen-Jack-Trey. Minni has led out with a 'donk bet' of 300 into a 900 pot, and Elon, the pre-flop raiser, still has to act behind me. This board texture hits many of the hands that would raise from the Button or call from the Small Blind. My Ace-high has very little equity here, and I have no significant draws. Continuing would mean floating with a weak hand while out of position against a player who has shown interest in the pot. The best play is to fold and wait for a better spot.

Pocket 8s is a strong but vulnerable hand on a Q-3-J rainbow flop facing a small lead from Minni after calling my preflop raise. The board features straight draws (e.g., 10-9, K-10) and possible top pairs (Qx, Jx). Minni's small bet sizing (300 into ~900 effective pot pre-action) suggests a wide range including draws, medium pairs, floats, or weak made hands, as well as value like QJ or top pair. Folding is too tight given my 6 outs to a set (~12% equity) and position. Raising risks bloating the pot OOP for her while isolating worse, but with deep stacks and her aggression history, she may defend wide or shove over. Calling is optimal: excellent pot odds (4:1), controls pot size, realizes equity in position, and allows turn flexibility (check back weak hands, bet/raise strong turns, or induce bluffs). Consistent with GTO vs. aggressive callers.

Turn pairs the J on board (Qd 3c Js → Jd). I have Qc6c: top pair but a very weak kicker. Elon opened pre and called my 1/3‑pot flop bet, so his calling range contains Jx (which just turned into trips), Qx, and some broadway/middle pair hands. Betting the turn here is thinly valueable and invites a check‑raise or large stacked commitment when I’m dominated (trip J) or outkicked. With pot = 1,500 and deep stacks behind, I want to control the size and avoid bloat with a marginal hand. I’ll check to keep the pot manageable and induce hands to bluff or show their strength. Plan: if Elon checks back we see a cheap river; if he fires a small bet (~≤1/3 pot) I can call and re‑evaluate on the river; if he bets large or puts in a check‑raise, I’ll fold—my kicker and hand texture don’t justify committing chips against that line.

Heads-up vs Minni on turn after she led small flop and now checks the Jd pairing the Js. Board: Qd-3c-Js-Jd features trips potential for Jx, possible QJ, straight draws (K10, T9, etc.), diamond flush draws, and overpairs vulnerable. My 88 is an overpair to deuces/3 but under any Q/J; blocks sets. Her small flop lead (polarized: value like QJ/Jx/draws or bluffs) into ~900, my call keeps pot controlled in position. Her turn check suggests caution: missed draws, medium strength fearing check-raise, or trapping trips/top pair. Betting ~2/3 pot (1000 into 1500) gets thin value from weaker pairs (77-TT, Ax high, Qx w/o kicker), charges draws (12-15% equity), denies equity, and represents strength given my button PFR + call. Overbet risks fold equity loss; checking pot controls but misses value realization vs worse/calling stations (Minni's aggressive history shows she defends). Position allows fold to raise if behind. EV+ to bet given range advantage post-pairing board.

Fold. The turn paired the J and Elon leads out for 1,000 into a 1,500 pot (pot is 2,500 with his bet) — calling 1,000 requires ~28.6% equity (1,000 / 3,500). I hold Qc6c: a marginal top pair with a very weak kicker and after the J on the turn I have no realistic backdoor flush (the turn was a diamond), only two queens left to make trips (2/46 ≈ 4.35%). Elon’s line (called my small flop bet, then a large turn bet) is exactly the line for Jx/trips, two‑pair/full house combinations or strong value hands, so my actual equity vs his betting range is far below the 28.6% needed. Raising would bloat the pot OOP with a poor hand and is too thin; calling just to see a river is a negative‑EV call given my tiny improvement chances. Preserve the stack and fold to this large turn bet.

I’m on the button with Q♦5♦ — suited queen with decent playability and positional advantage. With only the blinds to act and deep stacks, this is a good spot to open a wide stealing range. I’ll raise to 300 (3x the big blind) to apply pressure and either take the pot preflop or see a flop in position; the raise size is small relative to our stack but gives good fold equity. If I face a sizable 3-bet I’ll fold (Q5s fares poorly vs strong 3-bet ranges); if one or both call I’ll C-bet favorable textures and control the pot multiway.

With King-Four offsuit in the small blind, I am facing a standard 3x open from Minni on the button. While a King has some high-card value, K4o is generally too weak to defend against a button raise, especially out of position with the big blind still to act. Folding preserves my stack for better spots.

Facing a button raise to 300 (3x BB) from Minni, who has been aggressive in prior hands. My 9h4h is suited with straight and flush potential, solid playability against a wide button opening range (likely 30-50% of hands). BB defense strategy calls wide OOP vs late position opens due to position discount and implied odds. Pot odds are favorable: calling 200 into 450 (pot will be 650, ~31% equity needed, and 94s realizes ~38-42% vs typical button range). Deep stacks (~100bb effective) support speculative hands. Folding too tight; 3-betting risks too much with marginal hand. Call to realize equity and play postflop.

Flop board Qs Js Td is extremely draw-heavy: broadway straight draws (K9, 98), flush draws (spades), wheel draws, pairs with overcards. My 9h4h holds an open-ended straight draw (hits 8 for 89TJQ or K for 9TJQK), backdoor flush draw, and suited playability. Equity ~35-45% vs Minni's wide button opening range (overpairs like QQ+, AJ+, broadway, suited connectors, Ax suited). OOP as preflop caller, checking is optimal: pot control on coordinated board, induces bluffs/value from her frequent aggression (per hand history: often c-bets), allows check-raise strong hands or check-call floats/draws. Betting risks folding out worse and getting raised off equity; stacks deep (100+BB), multiway potential low since heads-up. Check to realize equity and exploit her range.

I raised preflop and now have top pair (Q♦) with a weak 5 kicker on a T♦ J♠ Q♠ flop. In position against one opponent this is a good spot to bet for thin value and to charge straight/flush draws while keeping the pot under control. The board is very connected, so I’ll size down to a half‑pot c-bet to extract from worse Qx, Jx and Tx hands and fold out air. If Elon check‑raises big I’ll assume stronger two‑pair/set/straight and fold; if he calls I’ll proceed cautiously on the turn.

Facing a c-bet of 325 into 975 on a highly coordinated QsJsTd flop, my 9h4h holds an open-ended straight draw (8 or K for broadway straight, 8 outs) plus backdoor heart flush draw and suited connector playability. Pot odds are excellent: calling 325 into 1300 (pot will be ~20% equity needed), and I have ~30-40% equity versus Minni's wide button continuation betting range (which includes overpairs, top pairs, flush draws, weaker broadways, and bluffs given her aggression in history). Deep effective stacks (~100BB) provide strong implied odds if I hit. Check-raising risks too much equity with a draw on this wet board where she can have straights (KT, AK), sets, or two-pair already, and folding out her floats/bluffs. Calling realizes equity OOP, keeps pot controlled, induces further bluffs on turn/river, and sets up potential check-raise if turn bricks or check-call if improves. Minni's history shows frequent aggression but also check-backs; exploiting by calling wide here maximizes EV in positionally disadvantaged spot.

Turn 7c is a relatively blank card for my hand, completing no immediate straights or flushes but low enough to possibly help some wheel straight draws (65, A5) in villain's range. My 9h4h retains the open-ended straight draw (8 or K for broadway, ~8 outs, ~17% equity to hit by river) plus backdoor heart potential, with no pair for showdown value. Equity remains solid (~25-35% vs Minni's continuing range post-flop call: top pairs like Qx/Jx, overpairs QQ+, AK/KT for straights, spade flush draws  K9s/AKs, 89, bluffs/air she floats with). OOP first to act with a semi-bluff draw and deep stacks (~95BB effective), checking is optimal: pot control prevents bloating pot with no made hand, induces bluffs/value bets from her aggressive button range (history shows frequent c-bets and turn barrels), allows check-call profitable bets (excellent pot odds on 1300 pot), check-raise protection if she overbets value, or check-fold if oversized. Betting OOP risks folding out worse (her floats/draws), facing raises from made hands (straights, sets), and poor EV with position disadvantage. Folding premature with live draws and implied odds. Prior flop call committed to realizing equity; checking continues balanced play, exploiting her aggression while controlling pot size.

I have top pair (Q♦) but a very weak 5 kicker on a highly connected flop (T♦ J♠ Q♠). The turn 7♣ is a harmless blank, but the board still has strong straight and flush possibilities in Elon’s calling range (AK = broadway straight on the flop, K9 combo straights, and spade draws). Elon called my small flop c-bet, then checked the turn — a range that includes worse Qx, Jx/TX pairs, draws, and a portion of very strong hands that will check‑raise for value. I want to: 1) extract thin value from worse Qx/Jx/TX hands, 2) charge spade and straight draws, and 3) keep the pot controllable with a vulnerable kicker. A half‑pot bet meets those goals: it gives value and denies free cards while not bloating the pot if I’m behind. I will bet ~650 into the 1300 pot. If Elon check‑raises large (polarized to two‑pair/straight), I’ll assume I’m beat and fold; if he calls, I’ll proceed cautiously on the river and re‑evaluate based on the card and his sizing.

Facing a turn bet of 650 into 1950 (~33% pot) on QsJsTd7c board with 9h4h holding an open-ended straight draw (8 or K for broadway, 8 outs ~17% to hit river) plus backdoor flush draw and suited connector playability. Pot odds require ~25% equity, which I have (~30-40% vs Minni's aggressive button range including top pairs, overpairs, unfinished spades draws like K9s/AKs, weaker broadways 89/KT bluffs, given her frequent c-bets/turn barrels in history). Deep stacks (95BB effective) offer massive implied odds if I hit (can stack vs worse straights like lower ends). OOP check-call optimal: realizes equity cheaply, induces further bluffs/value, controls pot without showdown value yet. Check-raising semi-bluff risks vs made straights (AK/KTs), two-pair (QJ etc.), folding out her bluffs/draws, poor post-OOP if flat-called. Folding -EV with live draw and history of her aggression (exploitable). Call preserves balance, maximizes EV.

River Jd pairs the Js, completing straights (e.g., 89, KT, AK), improving Jx to trips, and possible full houses (QJ unlikely without prior info). My 9h4h completely missed: gutshot (K) whiffed, no pair/flush/straight, pure air with 94-high. Called flop/turn OOP with ~17% equity + implied odds vs Minni's aggressive button range (frequent cbets/turn barrels per history), but brick eliminates showdown value/equity. OOP first to act, betting pure bluff -EV: exploits nothing (my line looks strong-ish speculative like missed draw/backdoor), faces calls from all made hands (trips+, straights, missed spades realizing value), raises crush stacks (~8900 eff). Check optimal: pot control, induces her value/bluffs (history aggressive, triple-barrel likely continues), check-fold vs bet (villain barrels wide but board favors value post-pairing), preserves stack for better spots late tournament (hand 97/150). No fold equity OOP, balance maintained by prior calls.

Board: Td Js Qs 7c Jd. My hand Qd5d is now only second pair with a very weak kicker. Elon cold‑called pre, called my small flop c‑bet and my turn bet — then checked the river. The river J is a terrible card for my holding: it gives any J trips/quads, turns QJ/QQ into full houses, and leaves many Broadway straights (AK, K9) and KQ ahead of me. Very few hands that are worse (mostly AT, some Tx) will reliably call a river bet, while a bet can easily be raised or shoved by hands that crush me. I’ll check and take this to showdown rather than bluff or thin‑value into a range that dominates me; if Elon fires a large bet I’ll strongly favor folding to his river aggression.

In 3-handed play, Q8o is a marginal opening hand from the button. While it is often an open in standard GTO charts, the current table dynamics involve a very aggressive chip leader (Minni) in the Big Blind who frequently defends and raises, and an active Small Blind (Elon). Given the goal is to maximize the stack over 150 hands and I currently hold a healthy 91 BB stack, I will opt for a disciplined fold to avoid a high-variance spot with a mediocre hand against aggressive opponents.

Heads-up in the small blind versus the big blind after the button folds. The pot is 150 and I only need to call 50 more for excellent pot odds (3:1). J4o is a playable hand with a live Jack, backdoor straight potential, and sufficient equity against Minni's likely wide defending range in the BB. Folding dead money would be too tight; raising light risks playing OOP post-flop with a marginal hand against an aggressive opponent like Minni who frequently calls and continues post-flop. Best to complete cheaply and realize equity on favorable flops.

I’m on the big blind with K♥7♠ and Elon (SB) has just limped. Heads-up vs a limp his range is wide, but I’ll be out of position postflop. K7s is marginal — good enough to see a flop but not strong enough to voluntarily build a bigger pot out of position. A raise to 200 would only add 100 more from me and still leave me OOP; I prefer pot control and to realize equity. I’ll check and plan to fold to strong aggression if I miss, call small bets to see turns, and only continue aggressively if I hit or get a favorable board.

Post-flop as OOP in SB with J4o on Kd Qd 2c board. Complete whiff: no pair, no flush draw (only one club), no straight draw (JQK with 4 doesn't connect). Pure air with jack-high showdown value at best against Minni's wide BB checking range. Board is relatively dry but features KQ high cards and diamond flush draw potential, favoring her likely holdings. No blockers to strong hands or draws. Betting would be pure bluff spew OOP with reverse position and poor playability. Checking extracts no value from better hands and allows folding to her bet, realizing equity only when ahead (rare). Minimizes losses with trash hand. Consistent with tight post-flop play against aggressive BB defenders like Minni observed in history.

Check. I have top pair with a weak kicker (K♥7♠) on K♦ Q♦ 2♣ and I’m out of position vs a limper with a wide range. The pot is small (200) and my hand is marginal to build a bigger pot OOP. Checking controls the size of the pot, avoids getting check‑raised into a tough spot, and lets me call small bets to realize equity. Plan: call modest continuation bets (~≤100, about 1/3–1/2 pot) and re-evaluate on the turn; fold (or strongly prefer folding) to large pot‑sized bets or check‑raises that represent two pair/sets or heavy diamond combos since my kicker is weak. If Elon checks back I’ll reassess the turn and look for safe spots to extract thin value or continue pot control if a diamond/overcard appears.

On the turn, the 10h completes my straight with J4, giving me T-J-Q-K for a strong made hand (K-high straight, second nuts behind A-high). Previously missed on flop with air, but now flopped open-enders or backdoors realized value massively. Minni's BB checking range vs SB limp is wide: pairs below KQ, Ax suited, suited connectors, bluffs/draws that missed KQd flop (heavy diamond draw board). She checked flop, suggesting no top pair or strong draw, but could float or have medium strength. OOP, betting extracts value from her worse pairs (TT-99, AQ), weaker draws (diamond flush draws that improve poorly), and any showdown value hands. Pot 200 is small relative to stacks; value bet ~3/4 pot (150) builds pot without scaring off callers while denying equity to draws (clubs or runner-runner straights). Folding/checking spews equity; overbet risks vs her big stack. Standard value line post-check-check.

Call 150. I have top pair (K♥7♠) but a weak kicker and I'm out of position vs a limp‑caller who checked the flop and now fires a small turn bet (150 into ~350). That sizing gives me ~23% pot‑odds to continue, and against his wide limp/call/check range my equity is comfortably above that (I beat Qx, many draws and air; I’m only behind to Kx, two‑pair/sets). Raising OOP with a marginal kicker would bloat the pot and put me in tough check‑raise/shove spots; folding is too tight given the price.
